identity.description: DIY investors managing their own portfolios of stocks and investments. They want control over their finances and plan for their own financial security. Could be employed, self-employed, or own a small to medium-sized business. Generally over 35 years old and homeowners.
influences: ShareScope writers: Maynard Paton, Richard Beddard, Bruce Packard, Michael Taylor
Stockopedia writers: Graham Neary, Roland Head, Edmund Shing

pain_points.primary_problem: Picking winning stocks and beating the market
secondary_problems: Information overload - deluge of data from the internet
Getting caught up in hype of 'hot stocks'
Navigating inflation, global economic uncertainty, and geopolitical instability
failed_solutions: Financial press (influenced by market makers and brokers)
Bulletin boards (full of BS and misinformation)
Waiting for uptrends and buying at the wrong time
Panic selling during corrections and getting whipsawed out

aspirations.dream_outcome: More winners than losers, better results than achieved before, and a feeling of being in control of their finances and their future
short_term_goals: Useful insights to navigate uncertain times
Guidance through inflation and economic uncertainty
Help understanding geopolitical impacts on markets
long_term_goals: Steady stream of advice and informed commentary
Topical suggestions for choosing sectors and individual stocks
Empowerment to DYOR (Do Your Own Research)

doubts: Family pressures from risk-averse spouse/partner or family members
Lack of spare cash to invest (inflation eroding spending power)
fears: Getting the risk/reward balance wrong for their stage of life
Making emotional rather than rational decisions
excuses: Waiting for confirmation before buying (missing early opportunities)
Talking up small successes instead of analyzing failures

problem_language: Too much emotion, not enough analysis
goal_language: Better returns than bank/building society accounts
venting_statements: I got burnt on that sector, I'm staying away
I missed the rebound because I was too scared to get back in
I keep talking about my winners but don't analyze what went wrong with my losers
I know I'm being too emotional and not analytical enough
